summary: Explosive Shakedown>Playing>China Doll sequence with soaring Dew
blurb: The second set erupts with a blistering Shakedown Street → Playing In The Band → China Doll suite, leading into a powerful Wheel space, Morning Dew and a jubilant Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s lead work shines on the jams while Bob steps up as the first‑set drags slightly. Crystal‑clear audience recordings make this one of the tighter 1983 Greek performances.
review: May 14, 1983 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre delivers a night that many fans still point to when they talk about the band’s early‑’80s peak. The first set opens tightly with “Feel Like A Stranger” and rolls through classics, though a few reviewers note it feels a touch ragged and even a sloppy moment on “Bertha”. The real fireworks ignite in the second set: a searing “Shakedown Street” that launches into a seamless “Playing In The Band” → “China Doll” suite, followed by a post‑drums Wheel space that bursts into another “Playing In The Band” jam, culminating in an emotional “Morning Dew” and a joyous “Sugar Magnolia”. Jerry’s guitar soars on the Wheel and Dew, with a few early‑set slips quickly forgotten as his solos tighten. Bob Weir supplies solid rhythm and vocal work, even stepping up when other parts wobble. Phil’s bass provides a steady, nuanced foundation throughout, while Brent’s keyboards, though low in the mix early, add atmospheric shading on the later jams. The dual‑drum attack from Mickey and Bill is highlighted by a tight pre‑drums jam and a crisp post‑drums space that fuels the Wheel. Across all recordings the audience tapes are astonishingly clear—several reviewers claim they sound like soundboard material—capturing every nuance without wash or boominess, making this show one of the most listenable Greek dates of ’83.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of nine recordings are positive, repeatedly praising the Shakedown>Playing>China Doll sequence, Wheel space, Morning Dew and overall tight rhythm section; three mixed reviews add minor criticisms but no consistent negative sentiment, justifying a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear audience tape that captures the explosive second set with superb detail, rivaling soundboard quality.

key_highlights: Incendiary Shakedown Street → Playing In The Band → China Doll suite in the second set
Soaring Wheel space leading into an emotional Morning Dew and jubilant Sugar Magnolia
Tight rhythm section with crisp drums, solid bass, and atmospheric keyboards
High‑fidelity audience recordings that sound like a soundboard

band_performance.Jerry: Razor‑sharp leads on Shakedown, Wheel and Dew; early set shows a few faster/sloppier moments but overall his soloing is soaring and tight.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, nuanced bass foundation that underpins the jams, especially evident in the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Steps up with strong rhythm guitar work and confident vocals, filling gaps when other parts slip; notable scat on Shakedown Street.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards sit low early but add atmospheric shading later, particularly on China Doll and the post‑drums space; overall mix is well balanced in most recordings.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums highlighted by an exciting pre‑drums jam and a tight post‑drums Wheel space that fuels the band's momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers tight, well‑balanced drumming throughout; no wash or boominess noted, contributing to the crisp overall rhythm section.
